steps.vulnerability_name: Set default no-store at nginx gateway
steps.description: Apply no-store/no-cache headers globally and override explicitly for public endpoints.

steps.vulnerability_name: Override products endpoint to short public cache
steps.description: Keep cacheability only for non-sensitive catalogue endpoint.
Defence in depth for direct-to-service traffic and future gateway drift.
Set no-store defaults at application layer in Django.

steps.vulnerability_name: Add CI contract test for cache-control
steps.description: Assert sensitive endpoints always emit Cache-Control: no-store.
steps.replace_code:
- name: Cache-Control must be no-store on sensitive endpoints
  run: |
    TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8888/identity/api/auth/login \
       -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
       -d '{"email":"test@test.com","password":"Password123!"}' | jq -r .token)

    for URL in \
      /community/api/v2/community/posts \
      /community/api/v2/coupon/validate-coupon \
      /workshop/api/merchant/contact_mechanic \
      /workshop/api/shop/orders/return_order ; do
      HDR=$(curl -sI "http://localhost:8888$URL" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | tr -d '\r' | grep -i '^cache-control:' || true)
      if ! echo "$HDR" | grep -qi 'no-store'; then
        echo "FAIL: $URL missing Cache-Control: no-store ($HDR)"; exit 1
      fi
    done

verification.description: Public products endpoint may use short cache
